claimText: The Dumpsite Remediation Accelerator Programme (DRAP) was launched under the Swachh Bharat Mission-Urban (SBM-U) 2.0 by the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA).
evidenceExcerpt: Launched under Swachh Bharat Mission-Urban (SBM-U) 2.0 by the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA).
examUse: Statement-based question on the nodal ministry and parent mission of DRAP.
distractorHooks: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC)

claimText: DRAP is a targeted initiative aimed at achieving the goals of Lakshya Zero Dumpsites by September 2026.
evidenceExcerpt: It is a year-long, targeted initiative to achieve the goals of Lakshya Zero Dumpsites by September 2026.
examUse: Statement-based question on the target year and specific goal of the programme.

claimText: Under DRAP, priority is given to sites with over 45,000 MT of legacy wastes, but there is no minimum threshold for Union Territories and North Eastern States.
evidenceExcerpt: Prioritizes sites containing over 45,000 MT of legacy wastes, with no minimum threshold for UTs and North Eastern States.
examUse: Statement-based question testing exceptions in policy thresholds for specific regions.
distractorHooks: Uniform threshold of 45,000 MT across all states and UTs
Minimum threshold applies only to North Eastern States

claimText: Biocapping and Biomining are key technologies used in DRAP, where Biocapping transforms dumpsites into green zones and Biomining uses microorganisms to extract economic materials.
evidenceExcerpt: Key technologies include Biocapping (transforming dumpsites into green zones) and Biomining (using microorganisms to extract economic materials).
examUse: Correct-pair or definition-based question distinguishing between Biocapping and Biomining.
distractorHooks: Swapping the definitions of Biocapping and Biomining
Biomining involves deep earth excavation for minerals
